title: Uplands Roast presents Tom Campbell Trio + Kyle Scott
location: The Bongo Club, 66 Cowgate; Edinburgh, EH1 1JX;  Scotland; United Kingdom
description:
We are back again for another end of year jambouree......

This time with a ceilidh, some pineapples and a selection of traditional instruments.

Moving from pure traditional vibes through to deep and heavy basslines - expect another mad one with all round house party vibes. 

This is not BYOP - pineapples will be supplied. 

***Tom Campbell Trio***

 The trio’s sound comes from a blend of tastes, ideas and experiences formed from Scottish and Irish musical traditions. A lot of the band’s music is original, although inspired by trad. An original threesome with modern styles and a fun and energetic vibe... Bringing us pure traditional vibes and a makeshift sound system club night ceilidh. 

***Kyle Scott***
Uplands Roast resident DJ - a dub techno specialist with an eclectic selection of dub inspired techno and house music. A firm favourite last year and back with a headline slot!!!

***Uplands Roast + Tom Campbell***
Tom and Stu (Uplands Roast Resident) have been playing together for 10 years - the promo clip for the event was recorded in 2017 at Mungo's HiFi's legendary Walk + Skank night. It was that reaction that kept them playing together and blending DnB + Bass music with a traditional flute.

***Botanica Sound***
Needs no introduction - taking the undergound music scene in Scotland by storm over the last few years. Present everywhere worth attending. Will will be coming to play B2B with the crew and bring the now staple Botanica inter-genre wobbles.

title: CandyFlip: OKO
location: The Bongo Club, 66 Cowgate; Edinburgh, EH1 1JX;  Scotland; United Kingdom
description:
CandyFlip are back at the Bongo club, bringing you the freshest and biggest artists shaping the scene. 

We’re kicking things off with an artist who’s taken over stages at Boomtown and Glastonbury, earned heavy support from BBC Radio 1, and shared lineups with the likes of Worship (Dimension, Sub Focus, Culture Shock, 1991) and Chase & Status. Sitting at the forefront of a new wave of rave-inspired sounds, this is one you don’t want to miss.

This is set out to be a big one so get in early!

--------------------

OKO is a Bristol-based DJ and producer known for her high-energy, rave-driven sound. Inspired by 90s classics and powered by modern club intensity, she blends drum and bass, techno, and hard dance into a distinctive style built for the dancefloor.

Her music has gained strong support from BBC Radio 1, with her latest single earning “Drop of the Week” on Charlie Tee’s show. With releases on Born On Road, Rinse, and High Tea Music, OKO continues to push her sound across the UK and beyond—supporting major acts like Worship and Chase & Status along the way.

On stage, her sets are fluid, fast-paced, and unforgettable. She’s performed at iconic venues and festivals including Printworks and Glastonbury, and most notably played the main stage at Boomtown to 33,000 ravers; cementing her status as one of the scene’s most exciting new artists.

OKO also runs Eight Five Two, a Bristol-based label and events brand championing community, culture, and authentic underground music.

With momentum building and her sound reaching wider audiences every month, OKO stands firmly at the forefront of a new wave of rave-inspired artists.

--------------------

On support we have local heavy weights M.O.B, Janverse, Rodent & Stitch for a full night of Drum and Bass and Jungle!

title: Haseeb Iqbal presents: Vin Gordon + Q&A
location: TBA - Cameo Picturehouse, null
description:
In an old stone village on the outskirts of Paris lives one of the most prolific reggae musicians in the history of Jamaica. Vin Gordon, the 75-year-old reggae titan, chooses to live an unassuming life. He spends his days walking in nature and teaching the trombone to young students, a world away from the hustle and energy of Jonestown in Kingston, Jamaica, where he made his name as one of the most impactful brass musicians to ever hail from the island.

With him, he keeps a catalogue of forgotten stories from an illustrious period of music history. As one of the only musicians to play extensively from the development of ska into rocksteady, and consequently reggae, Vin Gordon's legacy paints him as arguably the most featured musician in the legendary record label Studio One's history.

In this one-hour film, broadcaster, author and DJ Haseeb Iqbal traces down this overlooked figure and spends an afternoon with him in his humble apartment in rural France. They share conversation as Vin opens up about what he learned by playing in Bob Marley's band for 13 years. From the intricacies of Bob's character, to recording the legendary albums Kaya and Exodus together, Vin even reveals the trauma of being in the room during the assassination attempt of Marley.

Constantly accompanied by his trombone, Vin backs up stories with songs throughout, as Iqbal asks him to explore the never-discussed relationship between reggae and jazz. Vin's response, citing the influence of Sun Ra and John Coltrane bridges a gap hardly appreciated by journalists historically, as the pair celebrate how the African Americans and Jamaicans were united in the same struggle despite creating different sounds.

"The purpose of reggae music is to uplift people. But to remind them not forget the past too," he shares in response to one question.

This documentary is an uplifting and honest snapshot into the voice of one of reggae's forgotten heroes. One of the final living figures to play throughout the sound's evolution, Vin Gordon's stories bring to life a rich period of musical history that entails little digital footprint away from the music. It is a story that sheds light on a unique and magical period of sound. A period where sunshine music stemmed from struggle and healed the masses.

title: NonStopHyperpop✰
location: The Bongo Club, 66 Cowgate; Edinburgh, EH1 1JX;  Scotland; United Kingdom
description:
NonStopHyperpop
In 2024 we threw brat rave. Our long awaited excuse to run a hyperpop party after years of the genre being too niche in its infancy. In 2025 we called it on brat rave, but as one door closes, another opens.

You didn’t want it to end. So it doesn’t. 
Back by demand—but in a new form.

When Brat Rave concluded its chapter, it didn’t kill the energy. It left a gap.
This is what fills it. Glossy, chaotic, hook-heavy euphoria that hits instantly and never dips. NonStopHyperpop is here to pick that energy up and push it further.

No nostalgia bait, no soft launch. Just a full-throttle continuation of the sound that refuses to plateau.

From the hyper-saturated rush of SOPHIE and A. G. Cook to the chart-warping chaos of Charli XCX — this is the lineage.
But NonStopHyperpop is about where it lands on the dancefloor. The maximalist pop side of hyperpop, pushed to club pressure.

Expect:
Slayyyter, Ayesha Erotica, Shygirl, 100 gecs, Miss Kittin, Snow Strippers, Amara CTK100, Ninajirachi, MGNA Crrrta, Perfume, Bassvictim, Mayvi, Swordes, Pixy, Frost Children, Miss Luxury, Uffie, Charli XCX deep cuts, high-BPM euphoria, sugar-rush synths, nightcore-adjacent flips, and remixes that go harder than the originals
alongside the full Hyperpop spectrum from Indie Sleaze Electroclash to OG PC Music Bubblegum Bass. 

All killer no filler. All gas no comedown. Non. Stop. Hyperpop.

Known across Edinburgh's club scene as a techno DJ with a distinctive seamless DJing style, ona:v has a lesser-told side. EPiKA SOFT was born from her long-standing soft spot for electro, house, disco and all the deeper, warmer corners of club music - sounds she wanted to give more space to in Edinburgh.

description:
Vol. 7 of the Vinyl Listening Sessions delves deep into the history of music from London. From Jazz to Reggae to Punk, London's music scene is a melting pot of cultures that has acted as fertile ground for the development of some of the worlds most important genres.

Mark is one half of Greenhouse Records and host of The Pickle Community Radio. For this session they will be joined by Haseeb Iqbal, a visionary artist from London who's reshaping the city's DJ culture.

With a new theme each time, the listening sessions will focus on music that is not only beautiful to listen to but that also tells a story about the time and place in which it was made. Sit back, relax, and enjoy the music!

description:
Luke Solomon is undeniably one of British house music’s unsung heroes, with an impressive track record of underground success.

No-one could debate Luke Solomon’s dance music credentials. From setting up the seminal label Classic Recordings with Derrick Carter to taking quirky house to the UK Top 10 and international charts, with his band Freaks, to DJ'ing around the globe and performing live, Luke has been there for many of the more interesting 4/4 moments of the last decade.

Local house-head BETH joins him on the night.

Real DJs. No phones. No egos.

18+

title: Jacques Greene: Free Time x LUCKYME
location: Sneaky Pete's, 73 Cowgate; Edinburgh, EH1 1JW; Scotland; United Kingdom
description:
Jacques Greene has been making music about the club for over a decade. Across multiple EPs, two acclaimed full-length albums and a collaborative album with Nosaj Thing as Verses GT, his sound has evolved into an emotional, atmospheric haze that balances intimacy with physicality.

Beyond his own releases, he has collaborated with Bonobo, remixed Radiohead, produced for Jessy Lanza, arlo parks, Tinashe, and toured internationally with The xx, continually refining a sound that is both deeply personal and built for collective experience.

Greene also hosts a monthly residency on Rinse FM, now running for over three years, where he explores deep club music, new material and long-form mood-driven selections.

We're delighted to have him back this May for an early evening DJ set presented by Free Time and LUCKYME.

title: Borley Room: Jenn Gunn b2b gau7t
location: Sneaky Pete's, 73 Cowgate; Edinburgh, EH1 1JW; Scotland; United Kingdom
description:
Our May instalment sees us welcome two of Scotland’s finest garage acts go b2b for huge one in the Sneaks booth. 

Jenn Gunn has been a rising star in the Scottish club circuit for a number of years. Having supported the likes of Interplanetary Criminal, Soul Mass Transit System, and DJ Seinfeld as well as her own release on ec2a and her own radio show, she’s not one to miss. 

Gau7t is a Scottish super producer who made the move down to Glasgow last year. Having played at every club you can think of as well as his tracks being supported by Kettama, Skin on Skin, and Silva Bumpa to name a few. With releases on Nervous Records and ec2a as well as supporting huge names such as Ben UFO and Overmono, his stocks will continue to rise. 

Joining these two, BORLEY welcomes Liverpool wonderkid Baycantspin into the booth for his first ever Scottish appearance for their debut b2b. 

Expect house, garage, 4x4, breaks and everything inbetween.

title: SIGIL RE:BIRTH
location: People's Leisure Club, 45 Lothian Street, Edinburgh, EH1 1HB
description:
SIGIL RE:BIRTH 21/05/26

PYOTOR, THE GOO, NAIDONOVKA, T-TOTAL, OPEN DECKS

SIGIL has risen from the ashes and is now RE:BIRTHed – This underground club has finally returned with its People’s Leisure Club debut!
Expect all things trance related, undulating hypnotic sonic beats, baile, and everything else in between. 

PYOTOR
PYOTOR is an Edinburgh-based DJ and producer known for high-energy sets that cut across trance, bounce, and techno. Since picking up the decks in 2021, he's built a reputation for relentless speed and intensity on the dancefloor.

In early 2026, PYOTOR stepped into the production space, releasing his own music and cementing his presence beyond the booth. He is also the founder of Absolutely Nothing — a label and collective pushing the same uncompromising energy he brings to every set.

THE GOO
Producer and DJ the goo focuses on bringing euphoria to the dance floor through his eclectic mix of experimental electronica - blurring the lines between dance music and performance. Based between Brighton and Edinburgh, he’s been involved with hosting events like Augment and Mii Maker as well being a resident DJ at Janus

NAIDONOVKA
Born in Ukraine and shaped by an industrial mining landscape, naidonovka translates that environment into sound - raw, textured, dark, and ethereal.
Expect high-energy grooves, driving basslines and hypnotic, trance-like flow.
Part of KRYLA, a rave fundraiser for Ukraine, working as both organiser-promoter and DJ

T-TOTAL
T-Total: Recognised for his residency at EHFM with show Nightcap as well as promoter of the club night Silo, now known as SIGIL. Performed at Faerie Circle, Import, and Potpourri. Edinburgh based, T-Total pushes the boundaries of genre by enmeshing all things club, dub, trance, and techno

WHERE: People’s Leisure Club EH1 1HB

WHEN: 21st May 2026 11pm-3am

£6

title: Common Ground
location: Singh Street Cha, 13 Deanhaugh St, Edinburgh, , United Kingdom
description:
Common Ground returns for its second gathering, this time taking over Singh Street Cha, a speciality chai and coffee spot in the heart of Stockbridge.

Join us on Sunday morning from 10:00-12:00. Kids under 10 attend free when accompanied by an adult with a valid ticket.

To help us make the best use of the space and ensure as many people as possible can attend, last entry for pre-booked ticket holders is 10:45. After this time, we may begin offering any remaining spots to people at the door.

If space allows, your ticket will still be honoured after 10:45, but entry cannot be guaranteed.

We recommend arriving early to avoid disappointment.

Common Ground brings music into the daytime.

A space to show up, have a coffee or chai, move if you want to, or just take it in. No pressure, no expectations.

Our first event filled the room from start to finish, with a real mix of people, all sharing the same space through music.

This one builds on that.

title: Braw Gigs presents: Drew McDowall + Guests
location: The Bongo Club, 66 Cowgate; Edinburgh, EH1 1JX;  Scotland; United Kingdom
description:
More live action from Braw Gigs in May - and I’m super pleased to announce the return of legendary underground hero Drew McDowall, who some of you may have caught performing (in semi-secret!) at our Puce Mary gig in the Cowgate a few years ago or at Exit last year. Drew McDowall is a Scottish born, NYC based composer and musician. He was a member of Coil in the ‘90s contributing heavily to some of their most respected and influential works and is known for his diverse collaborations, working with Kali Malone, Catarina Barbieri, Varg, Puce Mary and LEYA amongst others.

His backstory reads like a primer of psychedelic fiction woven into statements of the unbelievable, superhuman, and outright insane. Growing up in the gangs of 1970’s Scotland, McDowall-fatigued by years of daily violence and the chaotic madness of that life- sought self-expression and sanctuary in punk and found a home in Glasgow’s rich underground music community.

After a stint with The Poems, a band he started with his then-wife Rose McDowall, he joined the ranks of UK avant-gardists Genesis P-Orridge, David Tibet, Peter “Sleazy” Christopherson, John Balance, and countless others who would come to define the industrial music's blossoming experimental vanguard. McDowall eventually collaborated with Psychic TV and became a full-time member of the hugely influential cult outfit Coil, where his influence shaped the group's later output: exercises in magical practice and music-as-psychoactive effect.

Support will come from one of London’s most exciting industrial noise acts, Welsh-born artist and musician Ekstasis began their brutal noise project at 19. Using chains, tape loops and modular synthesis as primary sonic outputs, with a debut release on Industrial Coast in 2024 and with countless shows and appearances in the last couple of years.

Rhins is the new project from Scottish based sound artist & composer J. Quimby. The album “The Burnout” was mastered by Lawrence English and released via Yann Novak’s Dragon's Eye Recordings in late April 2025. Rhins is drawn to and creates immersive sensory sound experiences that are extreme in contrast

description:
People's Disco Club Presents
AROOP ROY (2 hour set)
+ TONI McVEY
Friday 5th June
People's Leisure Club
11pm - 3am

Tickets: £8 - 12 + BF via RA

Aroop Roy is a DJ, producer and label head, who joins the dots between electronic music and borderless organic sounds. From house, garage and techno to jazz, disco and global rhythms, his productions have become a mainstay across scenes and generations.  He recently signed to Kerri Chandler’s Kaoz Theory and has forthcoming remixes for Basement Jaxx, Mr Scruff and Marcos Valle amongst many others. 

As a DJ, Aroop’s sets are full of personality and infectious groove, effortlessly blending sounds from across the musical spectrum and consistently bringing magic to the dancefloor. His all night shows and ‘Aroop Roy & Friends’ series have been immensely popular and he has played some classic sets at the likes of We Out Here,  Defected Croatia, Southport Weekender and Glitterbox.

Showcasing his skills as digger and tastemaker, Aroop's label - Vive La Musique - reissues and reworks rare eclectic sounds from around the world. From Caribbean Zouk and Congolese Rumba to French jazz-fusion and South African soul, the catalogue has been celebrated by tastemakers across the globe. 

“One of the most inspiring figures in club culture right now”
Clash Magazine

"Brilliant producer and incredible DJ. Aroop is someone who never fails!"
Gilles Peterson (BBC)

Support comes from our resident selector Toni McVey, known for her glittering all vinyl set spacked with classic disco energy.

Packed-out summer-soaked session incoming!!

title: Samedia presents DJ Rosa Pistola
location: The Mash House, 37 Guthrie Street, Edinburgh, EH1 1JQ, Scotland, United Kingdom
description:
DJ ROSA PISTOLA
+ Samedia DJs
+ Sol x PasosProhibidos in the bar

Saturday 6th June
The Mash House
11pm - 3am

Colombian native Rosa Pistola has always operated against the grain, creating her own platforms and pushing the boundaries of what club music means in her context. From her base in Mexico, she reconfigured the language of underground reggaeton, absorbing its rawness and erotic charge to project it into unexpected spaces. Now based in Spain, her work now goes way beyond these raw sounds that first brought her to global attention. Her versatility as a DJ and producer has led her to explore genres such as Guaracha, tribal, cumbia, merengue, funk, and dembow, fusing their codes without losing her identity.
Her ability to inhabit both the streets and the global circuit, to inject the intensity of perreo into environments that have historically ignored or underestimated it, has allowed her vision not only to resonate but to transform the way Latin American electronic music and its more experimental branches are understood.

Rosa's Scottish debut comes on the back of the release of her new ground-breaking album where she explores ancestry, tradition and indigenous folklore via her signature bass-driven underground club sound.  Titled Incorregible, the album is part of her global project, Tribal Sound System. It incorporates traditional instruments—such as Andean flutes, güiros, rain sticks and marimbas—into elements of regional Mexican music and contemporary electronics. Through the LP, the producer pays homage to Mexico’s tribes and draws inspiration from the pre-Hispanic sounds of the Americas.

We also welcome more new faces to the Samedia scene this time as Glasgow selectors SØL and PasosProhibidos take over in the bar while Samedia DJs support in the main room.

All welcome for a hot summer session in the Mash House!

Faro is a London-based DJ & artist known for soulful selections, vibrant energy, and a deep love for music. Since 2014, she's played across the UK & internationally at spots including SunceBeat, Kala Festival, We Out Here, Pikes Ibiza, and Fabric to name a few.

Rebecca Vasmant is a key figure in Glasgow's underground music scene - DJ, producer, composer & broadcaster blending spiritual jazz, broken beat, soul, hip-hop, Latin & African grooves.
A longtime resident and curator at Sub Club, with a monthly show on Worldwide FM and regular appearances on BBC Radio Scotland, she's become known for fearless selections and immersive live performances.
Her sold-out debut album With Love, From Glasgow - released on her own label Rebecca's Records - earned support from Gilles Peterson, Mr Scruff, Jamz Supernova & Huey Morgan, cementing her as one of the UK jazz scene's most exciting voices.
Whether behind the decks or leading her live ensemble, Rebecca moves seamlessly from deep electronic cuts to global soul and jazz-fuelled dancefloor energy.

We can't wait to see you on the dancefloor!

title: SIX40TWO aka JOHN NEWMAN : FREE TIME
location: Sneaky Pete's, 73 Cowgate; Edinburgh, EH1 1JW; Scotland; United Kingdom
description:
Free Time is Sneaky Pete's Sunday 6-10pm DJ series with legendary artists

___________________________________________ 

John Newman’s voice, songwriting and productions are already etched into the minds of millions, thanks to three UK No.1 singles, Brit Award and Ivor Novello nominations and a platinum-selling debut album. While those milestones made him a household name, they left him feeling trapped inside the brackets of fame and expectation, with little room to breathe.

SIX40TWO is the release. Newman’s new alias was born from his desire to rip a hole through those brackets and rediscover the fire that first drove him to make music - no rules, no boundaries, just energy and emotion. Named after the time his daughter was born, where his past work had focussed on fitting to the boundaries of commercial success, SIX40TWO follows the driving, danceable pulse of his creativity. “I wanted to respect this ambition for freedom inside me,” Newman says about the project. “I am now fully back in my creative element, and it is due to this project. I would class it as my saviour.”

This isn’t reinvention - it’s a homecoming. Pulling from the same deep roots that shaped Newman in the first place - soul, gospel and the darker textures of hip-hop - SIX40TWO reimagines it into something raw, physically, emotionally-charged and squarely for the club. It’s dance music that carries both the pressure and the release, the weight and the euphoria. “I’ve never changed the process,” Newman says. “I’ve just changed the sounds. My heart has always led in the same place.”

The first two singles capture the spirit of this new chapter. “Something in the Water” is a stormy, spiritual surge, built on heavy soul-esque sampled drums and crashing chords that feel like a distressed church sermon in a nightclub. Newman’s vocal sits between anguish and ecstasy as he sings, “It takes over my mind”, rising into a shimmering, sky-tearing drop. Thematically, it’s a raw confrontation with sobriety, identity and reinvention.

On the flip side, “What Would I Do”, is a bright, euphoric rush that holds onto joy with both hands, evoking golden-era French house represented with a slick, gothic atmosphere. Built around an addictive hook that feels like vintage Newman (“What would I do without your love?”), the track is propelled by uplifting and timeless synth chords that somersault through taut drums.

The journey back hasn’t been easy for Newman. In the past five years, he has stepped away from music to let the noise of fame fade so he could heal. But in that space, he reconnected with his younger self and landed back at dance music production, right where he first started.  Now, he continues to create what feels good to him without any responsibility - just pure drive and curiosity.

What began as a ground-up rebuild has spread to festivals across Europe and beyond, as Newman has played landmark shows at Tomorrowland Belgium and Brazil, Creamfields Chile, Exit Festival, Electric Love Festival, Beats for Love and Saga Festival. In 2025, Newman will hit Tomorrowland’s mainstage, Ushuaïa, AIA Sound Festival, Focus Festival and the F1 Belgian Grand Prix. Throughout it all, he continues to, in his words, “burst open the barriers and open the floodgates on new music”.

It gives us immense pleasure to welcome the legendary Grooverider to Leithienda for a very special set celebrating the music, culture, and energy of the Hacienda and the Summer of Love—the era that started it all.

Expect a journey through classic house, acid house and early rave sounds — the music that built the scene we all love today. 

A true pioneer. A true moment for Leithienda.

Music First. Always.

title: UpOnARoof Alfresco All-dayer Victor Simonelli + Iain Boney Clark
location: Skyline Sips, St James Quarter Rooftop, Level 5, St James Quarter, Edinburgh EH1 3AD
description:
WELCOME TO... UpOnARoof 3.0 @ SKYLINE SIPS

PRE REGISTER NOW

Tickets go live Sunday 10th May at 2pm.

Join us on Level 5 of St James Quarter for a semi-alfresco rooftop escape, boasting breathtaking views over Edinburgh's iconic Calton Hill.

After two completely sold-out events in 2025, UpOnARoof returns — bigger, better, and bringing another phenomenal lineup to the city.

This edition, we're proud to welcome back a true legend...

VICTOR SIMONELLI - HERITAGE NEW YORK STYLE SET

A global force in house music, Victor Simonelli is one of the original pioneers of the New York underground scene. Known for his relentless touring schedule and timeless sound, Victor continues to shape dance floors worldwide with both classic cuts and fresh productions.

From his early work with Sub-Urban Records, alongside Tommy Musto under the iconic TMVS moniker, to producing classics like "Don't Be Shy" and Federal Hill's "I Have Something For You", Victor has built a legacy that spans decades.

He later launched influential labels including Bassline, Big Big Trax, and more recently Stellar and West Side, showcasing both his own productions and emerging talent.

An unmatched musical résumé
Victor's production, mixing, and editing credits read like a "who's who" of music history. He has worked with legendary artists including:
David Bowie, Michael Jackson, James Brown, Quincy Jones, Talking Heads, Deborah Harry, and many more.

In the world of dance music, he's collaborated with icons such as Jocelyn Brown, Robert Owens, Martha Wash, and Gwen Guthrie — helping define the sound of a generation.

A mentor and innovator
Victor has also played a key role in nurturing new talent, notably mentoring Sandy Rivera (Kings of Tomorrow), further cementing his influence on the evolution of house music.

WITH SUPPORT FROM:

West Coast Streetrave / AFROBOOGIE legend Iain "Boney" Clark, bringing decades of experience and going a little different digging into his more soulful eclectic side.

Plus a strong lineup of local talent:
James Combe
Dave Fergusson
Jean Lawrie
Dave Elders

Expect:
• Authentic NYC house, disco & everything in-between
• A stunning rooftop setting
• An electric crowd and atmosphere
• Another unforgettable UpOnARoof experience

This is more than just a party — it's a celebration of house music heritage, past, present, and future.

FOOD AVAILABLE TO ORDER TO TABLE ON THE DAY

Growing from venue to venue, with a brief pause over lockdown where livestreaming became the norm, 2026 marks Shadowplay's 10th birthday! Now focused mostly on newer bands and styles, with a nod to the trailblazers, the club looks towards coldwave, deathrock and synthpunk - anything that promotes that dark punk spirit.
No Spotify playlists, no costume competitions, just real DJs playing great tunes.

For this event, we descend upon Devil's Cut, the exclusive speakeasy underneath Angel's Share hotel, for a special night celebrating this gothic legacy. Stay tuned for some surprises!


Check out previous sets at shadowplayedinburgh.co.uk to see what kind of night it is – including what was played at events over the last 10 years.  I highly recommend pre-buying and getting down early for a great night of music!
Tickets through ra.co - limited run of discount tickets for those who remember our first venue...

Shadowplay Edinburgh is one of the Scottish capital’s freshest and most innovative night playing the classic sounds of goth; post-punk, synthpunk, deathrock, dark wave, touching both classics and touching on more contemporary bands and styles. With a DIY ethic and a passion for dark alternative music, Shadowplay is growing and becoming an integral part of the UK goth scene.

Poster photography by @deadpan.photos

title: Leithienda - Dave Angel - Techno Special

description:
THE RAVE NEVER STOPPED #2

DAVE ANGEL

From the Acid House explosion of the late 80s to dominating dancefloors worldwide, Dave Angel has been at the forefront of the sound, blending Detroit influence with raw UK energy.

A DJ who’s played historic Scottish events like Technodrome and Pure, now lands in Leith, his first time playing in the Scottish capital for many years. With his debut album set for release on R&S Records in the autumn, we are extremely proud to welcome back this legendary DJ to Edinburgh.

A true pioneer of UK Techno and Rave culture.

In support, we have Edinburgh’s finest purveyor of Techno, Stephen Brown.

Proper history. Proper energy. No gimmicks — just serious Techno and Rave Roots.

Music First. Always.

title: NO FATE - Synthwave/EB(S)M/Cyberpunk
location: People's Leisure Club, 45 Lothian Street, Edinburgh, EH1 1HB
description:
A night of neon synth sounds; a bleak look at the future through the lense of the past – coloured by the 1980s vision of a post-apocalyptic dystopia where we almost find ourselves today. The Yamaha-drenched sounds of recent years, with the Cubase-influenced heavy beats of today will provide the soundtrack, while DJ Vilkas (Shadowplay, Fimbulvetr, AbSynth) & special guest PUCK (Idlestates) entertain with images from prophetic visions of the future.

Ethan Anderson, AKA Puck, is the founder and creative force behind Idlestates Recordings, an imprint rooted in Scotland, UK.
Emerging in the early 2020s, he developed Idlestates as a platform for experimental electronic music driven by a punk leaning ethos—bringing together the raw edges of electro, EBM and industrial. This identity runs through the label's releases, his DJ sets, and an evolving archive of radio broadcasts.
As both contributor and curator across the labels releases, he has connected a diverse group of rising and established artists, united by a commitment to raw, boundary pushing sound. His vision continues to steer Idlestates as it broadens its catalogue and global community, reinforcing the label’s reputation as a home for forward thinking electronic innovation.

DJ Vilkas co-founded Shadowplay Edinburgh in 2016, playing the new sounds of goth, coldwave, postpunk and deathrock in the capital. This industrial-themed spinoff, NO FATE, is now in its seventh annual incarnation. Usually reserved for Twitch and YouTube-based livestreaming, 2026 marks the third real life excursion.

An evening of music, film and appreciation of a particular scene in a particular time. Join us for the soundtrack to the apocalypse, as 1982 imagined in 2026. Check out the website for past sets and what you might expect this time.

Friday 31 July 2026
People's Leisure Club, Lothian Street, Bristo Square, Edinburgh
tickets via ra.co
Right Of Entry Reserved - this isn't a fancy dress contest, but please respect the theme of the event and dress darkly - all black minimum or some kind of cyber/goth themed attire will be expected at the door.

title: Maribou State
location: TBA - Fringe by the Sea Big Top , null
description:
UK electronic duo Maribou State bring their soulful, genre-blurring sound with their stunning, immersive live show. 

Chris Davids and Liam Ivory have spent over a decade crafting a warm, organic style that blends laidback electronica with touches of soul, UK club music and rich atmospheric sounds.

Since emerging in 2011, Maribou State have become one of the most exciting names on the global dance scene. From their breakthrough album Portraits to the acclaimed Kingdoms in Colour, and their recent album Hallucinating Love, their music balances evocative samples with emotional depth, creating tracks that feel equally at home on the dancefloor or on a festival field.

Known for their immersive live performances, the duo have played everywhere from Glastonbury to the Sydney Opera House, selling out tours across the UK, Europe and North America. Their live shows combine live instrumentation, soaring vocals and deep, groove-led electronics to create an unforgettable atmosphere.

Expect smooth grooves, expansive beats and a feel-good energy as we welcome Maribou State to Fringe by the Sea.

Support act to be announced.
Under 18s are welcome. Under 16s must be accompanied by an over 18.

title: Pulse: Surgeon
location: The Bongo Club, 66 Cowgate; Edinburgh, EH1 1JX;  Scotland; United Kingdom
description:
Surgeon has been at the forefront of UK techno since 1994.
His debut EP is regarded by many as seminal, launching his career along with several early Downwards releases, causing a ripple of excitement through the techno cognoscenti of the time. Surgeon has been prolific since, with three albums on Tresor and numerous Counterbalance and Dynamic Tension EPs as well as his 'Breaking The Frame’, ‘From Farthest Known Objects’ and ‘Luminosity Device’ LPs “Over the course of his career, the Birmingham resident has perfected a unique and uncommonly effective production style. His is a tough techno sound with an industrial murk about it but also plenty of funk, swing and a sophisticated sense of dub-space learned in part from his Chain Reaction contemporaries in Berlin. Proving that hard can also be smart, sensuous and danceable, the influence of Surgeon on the 2010-ruling Ostgut/Berghain sound – DJs/producers like Marcel Dettmann, Shed et al – is substantial”. (Fact Magazine)

title: Feel The Real: Rap Saunders, Frederick
location: People's Leisure Club, 45 Lothian Street, Edinburgh, EH1 1HB
description:
We’re bringing that Soulmates vibe to Edinburgh—deep, soulful, and built for real house music lovers—known for their welcoming crowd, spiritual energy and deep house foundations—this night is all about quality sound, no compromise.

Joining Feel The Real resident, Frederick is Rap Saunders, a true selector and cornerstone of the UK house scene. With decades behind the decks, his sets are a journey—rooted in soul, shaped by the underground, and driven by pure feeling. Expect depth, groove and moments that lock the room in from start to finish.
